originalTitle: South Africa's long-serving former defence minister and former Robben Island prisoner dies
title: Remembering a Brave Hero: Mr. Lekota
content:
A Sad Day in South Africa

Hello, friends! Today, we have some news from a beautiful country in Africa called South Africa. Sometimes, news can be a little sad. Today, we heard that a very special and brave man named Mr. Mosiuoa Lekota has passed away. When someone passes away, it means they have gone to rest forever. It makes us feel sad, but it also makes us remember all the good things they did during their life. Mr. Lekota was a very important person in South Africa. He helped his country become a better place for everyone. Even though we are sad, we can also think about how brave and kind he was. He lived a very long life and did many wonderful things for the people of South Africa. We will learn more about his amazing journey.

Who Was Mosiuoa Lekota?

Mr. Mosiuoa Lekota was a true hero. He was a leader who cared deeply about all people. Imagine someone who always wants to make sure everyone is treated fairly and kindly. That was Mr. Lekota! He worked very hard to make sure everyone in his country could live happily and safely. He was known as 'Terror' to his friends when he was young, but not because he was scary! It was because he was so energetic and brave. He was always ready to stand up for what was right. He believed that every single person should be treated with respect, no matter their skin colour or where they came from. He wanted everyone to be friends. Did you know? South Africa is a country at the very bottom of the continent of Africa! It has amazing animals like lions and elephants, and beautiful beaches. It is a land of many different people and cultures.

A Time of Trouble: Apartheid

Long, long ago, in South Africa, there were some very unfair rules. These rules were called 'Apartheid.' It's a big word, but it just means that people were not treated equally. Some people were treated badly because of the colour of their skin. They couldn't go to the same schools, or live in the same places, or even play in the same parks as others. It was a very sad time. Many people felt hurt and angry because of these unkind rules. They wished for a world where everyone could be friends and share everything. Mr. Lekota and many other brave people did not like these unfair rules at all. They wanted things to change. They believed that all people are equal. They knew it was wrong to treat anyone badly. They spoke out and said, "This is not fair!" They wanted peace and kindness for everyone. This took a lot of courage and strength.

Brave Leaders Like Mr. Lekota

Because Mr. Lekota and his friends wanted to change the unfair rules, the people who liked the bad rules got angry. They didn't want things to change. So, they put Mr. Lekota in prison. It means they locked him up because he was fighting for what was right. This was very brave of him. He was not scared to stand up for what he believed in. He knew that even though it was hard, it was important to keep fighting for kindness and fairness. He wanted South Africa to be a place where everyone smiled and felt safe. He spent many years in prison for his beliefs. But even in prison, he never gave up hope. He kept dreaming of a day when all children in South Africa could play together, go to the same schools, and be treated like brothers and sisters.

The Island Prison: Robben Island

Mr. Lekota was sent to a very famous prison called Robben Island. It was a prison on a small island, far away from the land. Imagine being on an island surrounded by big ocean water! It must have been a lonely place. Robben Island was a very difficult place to be. But many brave people, who wanted fairness and freedom for everyone, were kept there. They were all heroes, just like Mr. Lekota. Did you know? An island is a piece of land with water all around it! Some islands are big, and some are very small. Robben Island is near the city of Cape Town in South Africa.

Friends with Nelson Mandela

Guess who else was in that prison? A very, very famous hero named Nelson Mandela! Mr. Lekota and Nelson Mandela were friends in prison. They shared their hopes and dreams for a better South Africa. They talked about how they would change the country once they were free. Nelson Mandela was a very kind and wise man. He taught everyone about peace and forgiveness. He was a shining light for all of Africa and the world. Being friends with Nelson Mandela showed just how special Mr. Lekota was. They both knew that even though they were locked up, their ideas of fairness and kindness could never be locked away. Their message of hope traveled all over the world. They inspired many people to never give up on doing what is right. Did you know? Nelson Mandela became the first Black president of South Africa after the unfair rules of Apartheid finally ended! He showed everyone how to forgive and build a new, kind country.

After Prison: Helping His Country

Finally, after many years, the unfair rules of Apartheid ended. Mr. Lekota and Nelson Mandela and all the other brave people were set free! They could go home and help build a new, fair South Africa. It was a day of great joy and celebration! Mr. Lekota didn't stop helping his country. He became a very important helper for the president. He was a 'minister.' A minister is like a special helper in the government who takes care of important things for the country. He even became the 'defence minister.' That means he helped make sure South Africa was safe and protected. He worked hard to make sure everyone had peace and security. He wanted all children to grow up safe and happy.

What We Can Learn

Mr. Mosiuoa Lekota showed us what it means to be truly brave. He taught us to always stand up for what is right, even when it is hard. He taught us to believe in fairness and kindness for everyone. His life was a big, beautiful lesson in courage and hope. When we think of Mr. Lekota, we remember to be kind to our friends, to share our toys, and to make sure everyone feels included. Because of brave people like him, South Africa is now a country where everyone is equal. Let's remember Mr. Lekota's kindness and bravery. We can all try to be heroes in our own small ways, by being kind to others and standing up for what is fair. That is the best way to honor his memory. Thank you, Mr. Lekota, for everything you did. Did you know? Even small acts of kindness, like sharing your snack or helping a friend, can make a big difference in the world! You can be a little hero too!

originalTitle: Israeli air attack hits Beirut during live report
title: A Big Sound in a Faraway City
content:
Hello, My Little Friends!

Hi everyone! It's me, your friendly African news reporter! I'm here to tell you about something that happened far, far away. Sometimes, things happen in the world that grown-ups need to know about. And sometimes, you, my little friends, might hear about them too. My job is to tell you these stories in a way that is easy to understand. And most important, to help you feel safe and calm. We always want to be safe, right?

What is a News Reporter?

Do you know what a news reporter does? Imagine someone who loves to tell stories. They go to different places. They see new things. And then they tell everyone what happened! They use their voice, or write words, or take pictures. This helps us all learn about our big, big world. Reporters help us know about special animals, amazing discoveries, and sometimes, sad things too. But they always try to tell the truth. They are brave people! **Did you know?** News reporters travel all over the world to find out what is happening! They can be in a hot desert or a cold snowy place.

A City Called Beirut

Let's talk about a city far, far away from us. It's called Beirut. Can you say "Bay-root"? It's a beautiful city in a country called Lebanon. Lebanon is in a part of the world called the Middle East. Beirut is a big city, just like some big cities in Africa. It has tall buildings and houses. It also has a lovely sea right next to it! Imagine living by the big, blue sea. Lots of people live there. They go to school, play with friends, and eat yummy food, just like you. **Did you know?** Beirut is the capital city of Lebanon! A capital city is like the main office for a whole country.

Zeina, the Brave Reporter

In Beirut, there was a very brave lady. Her name is Zeina Khodr. Can you say "Zay-na Co-der"? Zeina is a news reporter, just like me! She works for a TV channel called Al Jazeera. Her job is to tell stories from Beirut and the places nearby. One day, Zeina was doing her job. She was standing outside. She was talking to the camera. This is called a "live report."

What is a Live Report?

Imagine you are talking to your friend on a video call. When you talk, your friend hears you right away, right? That's kind of like a live report! When Zeina was talking, people all over the world could see and hear her right at that very moment. She was telling everyone what was happening in Beirut. This is how news travels super fast! It helps everyone know what is going on quickly. **Did you know?** When a reporter is "live," it means you are seeing and hearing what they are doing *right now*!

A Big, Loud Sound

So, Zeina was talking live to the camera. She was telling her story. And then, something happened. She heard a very, very loud sound. A big "BOOM!" sound. Imagine a big firework going off, but even louder. This sound came from a place nearby. It was in the southern part of Beirut. This part is called the "suburbs." Suburbs are like parts of a city that are a little bit outside the very center. Like neighborhoods that are connected to the main town.

What Was That Sound?

The loud sound Zeina heard was from something called an "air attack." This is a grown-up word for when things happen in the sky that make loud noises. Sometimes, sadly, countries have disagreements. And sometimes, these disagreements can cause big sounds. It's a sad thing when countries are not friends. The sound Zeina heard came from something happening between Israel and Beirut. Israel is another country, close to Lebanon. **Did you know?** Countries usually try to be good friends and help each other. But sometimes, like with people, they have problems.

Zeina's Calm Face

Even though there was a loud sound, Zeina stayed very, very brave. You could see her face. She kept telling her story. She didn't run away. She wanted to make sure everyone knew what was happening. Her job is to report the news, even when it's a bit scary. She showed us how brave news reporters are. It must have been a little bit scary for her, but she kept going.

Why Do We Hear This News?

It's important to know about things that happen in the world. Even if they are far away. When we hear about big sounds and problems, it reminds us that grown-ups are always working for peace. Peace means everyone is safe and happy. No loud sounds, no worries. Just friends playing and families laughing. When problems happen, people hope that things will get better soon. We all wish for peace for everyone, everywhere. **Did you know?** Many people all over the world hope for peace and try to help people who are having a hard time.

Talking About Our Feelings

Sometimes, when we hear about big, loud sounds or problems in the world, it might make us feel a little bit sad. Or confused. Or even a little worried. And that's okay! It's normal to feel these things. If you ever hear something that makes you feel worried, please, please talk to a grown-up you trust. Talk to your mom, your dad, your grandma, grandpa, or your teacher. They can help you understand. They can give you a hug. They will tell you that you are safe.

Staying Safe at Home

Remember, my little friends, this happened very far away from us. Your grown-ups work very hard to keep you safe every single day. You are safe in your homes. You are safe at your schools. We just want to share stories from around the world so we can learn and grow. And remember to always hope for kindness and peace everywhere. **Did you know?** There are many, many good people in the world who work hard to make sure everyone is safe and happy.

Let's Hope for Peace

So, Zeina, the brave reporter, heard a loud sound in Beirut. She kept doing her job. We learned about a city called Beirut and a brave reporter. And we remember that peace is a wonderful thing. Let's all think good thoughts for the people in Beirut. Let's hope that things become calm and quiet there very soon. Thank you for listening, my little friends! Stay safe and keep learning! This is your African news reporter, signing off!
summary: A brave news reporter named Zeina Khodr was talking live from a city called Beirut when she heard a very loud sound.
whyMatters: It's important to learn about different places and hope for peace and safety for everyone in the world.
